_comment: Layout manifest — the SINGLE source of truth for layouts. The server DERIVES each layout's route as /<id> (route is NOT a field; the title is display-only) and serves its HTML/JS/CSS; the chooser builds its cards from this. active:false hides the chooser CARD (and omits the layout from /layouts.json) but its route /<id> STAYS reachable by direct URL — so a new layout can be verified before activation; flip active:true to surface the card. See docs/ADDING_A_LAYOUT.md. Add a layout here and it surfaces in BOTH the chooser and the routes (no server-side edit). id must be URL-safe. kind: function-mapped | generic | placeholder. category is a declared grouping label. generic:true marks a MODEL-AGNOSTIC controller: its channel map ships UNMAPPED (slot/channel null) and the layout shows an auto-assign wizard on first use; it still resolves function->channel like any function-mapped layout (orthogonal to kind). card:false hides the chooser CARD while keeping the route + the /layouts.json entry (so it still shows in the layout dropdown); devIcon:true surfaces the layout as a small icon in the startpage top menu instead of a card (RAW: a debug wrench).

layouts.name: Brick controller
layouts.description: Model-agnostic brick-style gamepad — two sticks, two tracks, buttons. Auto-assign to any toy.

layouts.name: 12-axis controller
layouts.description: Model-agnostic gamepad — two sticks, two tracks, buttons. Auto-assign to any toy.

layouts.name: Bring your own
layouts.description: Build a layout for your own toy — and share it.

layouts.name: TEMPLATE — your toy here
layouts.description: Inactive starter for a function-mapped layout. Copy it, give it a unique id, and flip active:true. See docs/ADDING_A_LAYOUT.md.
